scholarships.name: PEEF - Punjab Educational Endowment Fund
scholarships.overview: The Punjab Education Endowment Fund (PEEF) has announced scholarships for Master's Level Degree Programs for the Academic Year 2025-26. All students enrolled in Master's Level Programs (Fall 2025 & Spring 2026 semester intake only) are eligible to apply for the subject scholarship.
eligibilityCriteria: Punjab domicile

Secured at least 60% marks or a minimum CGPA of 2.5 in graduation-level examinations held in 2023-24, 2024-25, or 2025-26 (Fall/Spring sessions) from Punjab (minimum sixteen years of schooling or 4-year education after HSSC / F.A / F.Sc / Grade 12 examination)
Secured admission as a full-time student in the current academic year 2025-26 (Fall 2025 & Spring 2026 Intake) in one of the partner universities/degree-awarding institutions
Students enrolled in fall/spring sessions (where applicable) and morning/afternoon/evening sessions (where applicable) are eligible to apply
Declared monthly income of parents (from all sources) is equal to or less than Rs. 60,000/-
Children of Govt. employees in BPS 1-4 are exempted from the monthly income condition if salary is the only source of income
Children of civilians martyred in terrorist attacks are exempted from the monthly income condition
Not availing any other educational scholarship during the current academic year
Besides fulfilling PEEF eligibility criteria, the prospective student must qualify the merit and need criteria of the respective university/degree-awarding institution
Students who have already passed/possess MA/MSc/MS/MPhil or any equivalent degrees are ineligible to apply
Universities have the right to cancel the scholarship in case of wrong or incomplete information
Application forms should only be submitted to the respective institution; PEEF will not entertain applications submitted directly to the PEEF Office
applicationProcess: Copy of PEEF form (after submission at the university office)
PEEF income affidavit stamp paper
Copy of Punjab domicile
Certified copy of previous payslip or pension book (for govt./private servant or retired govt. servant)
Copy of graduation result
One passport-sized photograph with a blue background
Copies of utility bills
Copies of CNIC of both mother and father

scholarships.name: National Outreach Program (NOP)
scholarships.overview: NOP scholars benefit from the multicultural environment, capable faculty, enlightening studies, rich campus environment and exceptional and prestigious NOP-focused enhancement opportunities at LUMS to build a solid foundation for life-long learning and success. Joining the most rigorous university in the country and then the most rigorous program at that university, NOP students ascend to the pinnacle of higher education in the country. This personal transformation and experience form the foundation for their social contributions: today, NOP alumni are spread across continents and form a cadre of impactful change-makers who are an asset to their communities and to the entire nation.
eligibilityCriteria: At least 80% marks in Matric or equivalent in O-Levels
Financial need
Students shortlisted based on the above criteria are invited to the NOP Summer Coaching Session, where they will attempt different exercises and tests
Students are evaluated on their performance at the Summer Coaching Session and are registered for the entrance test, which is completely paid for by LUMS
Finally, students who apply to LUMS and qualify on merit, are offered admission on full scholarship
All students, irrespective of whether they apply directly or through the NOP programme, are evaluated on the same admission criteria

While assessing financial need, a review committee ensures transparency in the process of awarding scholarships to all those who qualify for admission (this review is conducting annually during a student's study tenure)
applicationProcess: The NOP team visits schools/colleges in various cities, towns and villages all over Pakistan to give presentations about the LUMS National Outreach Programme
Students send their applications for the NOP Summer Coaching Session to LUMS and the shortlisted students are invited on campus in the summer to experience university life at LUMS
Students hoping to attain the NOP scholarship apply for admission to LUMS along with other regular students
Students take the Admissions Test, which is funded by LUMS
Based on the financial need assessment, admitted students are offered a fully-funded scholarship in the LUMS undergraduate programmes

scholarships.name: Northern University Zakat-Based Scholarship
scholarships.overview: The Northern University Zakat-Based Scholarship provides financial support to students from underprivileged and low-income families. Funded from Zakat, this scholarship ensures that deserving students can continue their education without financial hardship. The scholarship is aligned with the principles of social welfare and Islamic charitable giving.
eligibilityCriteria: Applicants must demonstrate financial need
Must provide proof of family income or financial hardship
Must be enrolled or admitted to an eligible program at Northern University
Scholarship is awarded annually and may be renewed based on continued financial need and academic standing
applicationProcess: Submit Zakat scholarship application to the Financial Aid office
Provide all required financial documents
The Zakat committee reviews applications and awards scholarships based on financial need
Students are notified of the award decision via email

scholarships.name: Northern University Merit-Based Scholarship
scholarships.overview: The Northern University Merit-Based Scholarship is awarded to outstanding students on the basis of academic excellence. The scholarship aims to encourage high-achieving students by providing financial assistance to support their higher education at Northern University.
eligibilityCriteria: Applicants must have excellent academic performance in previous examinations
Scholarship is awarded strictly on merit
Students must be enrolled or admitted to an eligible program at Northern University
Students must maintain the required GPA/CGPA to continue receiving the scholarship
applicationProcess: Apply for admission at Northern University
Merit-based scholarship consideration is carried out automatically or through the admissions office
Shortlisted students are notified by the university

scholarships.name: Need-Based Scholarship (Northern University)
scholarships.overview: Northern University offers need-based financial support to students from underprivileged backgrounds to ensure access to higher education.
eligibilityCriteria: Financial need
Enrolled student at Northern University
applicationProcess: Scholarship details are handled internally by the university administration.
